summary: This is a very brief teaser post promoting Joel's April column in Inc. Magazine. It opens with a colorful anecdote about parachuting from a plane, referencing his military experience. Joel promises this will be the last time he tells 'shaggy dog stories' about his army days. The post is essentially a plug for his Inc. Magazine column with minimal standalone content.
key_points: Joel was writing a regular column for Inc. Magazine aimed at small business owners
He frequently drew on his military service as a source of analogies and stories for business lessons
The post acknowledges his tendency toward lengthy, roundabout anecdotes ('shaggy dog stories') and promises to move on from military references
money_quotes: If the flight attendants on the JAL 747 from Tokyo I'm on right now were to, in a remarkable lapse of Japanese standards of service, throw me off the plane with a parachute, I could do a pretty nice roll when I hit the ground.
key_insight: This is a lightweight teaser post directing readers to Joel's Inc. Magazine column, notable mainly for its characteristic storytelling style and self-aware humor about his reliance on military anecdotes.
